running_but_low: test_set (timing out, hundreds of tests running)
test_tuple (timing out, dozens of tests running)

test_binop.notes: Rat fraction class equality tests fail

test_unary.notes: test_no_overflow needs eval+bigint; test_bad_types needs TypeError on +b'a'

test_list.notes: errors are test_iterator_pickle and test_reversed_pickle (pickle not supported for list iterators); skipped is @cpython_only test_preallocation

test_dict.notes: Pickling tests (6), __dict__ mutation, str subclass issues, exec(), hash-based key equality

test_set.notes: Many tests running; needs longer timeout to get full count

test_tuple.notes: Many tests running; some hash-distribution tests are slow

test_string.notes: Fixed by class-scope name resolution (methods no longer see sibling methods), PEP 570 positional-only+**kwargs binding, and TypeError for arg-count errors. Remaining: get_identifiers (regex named groups), auto_numbering, format-spec padding, Template col/is_valid.

test_bytes.blocker: Check for hidden control characters or use proper encoding

test_exceptions.notes: No longer crashes with a Go stack overflow: the interpreter now enforces sys.getrecursionlimit() (default 1000) and raises RecursionError. support.Py_TRACE_REFS stub added. Remaining errors are deeper exception/traceback features.

test_with.notes: Runs. 22->25 when `with cm as obj.attr:` / `as lst[0]:` stopped wrongly unpacking (UnpackPattern now treats a (. ...)/get-item S-expr as a single assignable target). Remaining: deeper context-manager protocol/exception-propagation behavior.

test_call.notes: Runs; 180 skipped (need _testcapi). ** unpacking now accepts any mapping (keys()+__getitem__), fixing test_kwargs_order for OrderedDict. Remaining errors: compile(optimize=) kwarg, 500-arg function.

test_functools.blocker: error evaluating dict key: NameError: name '**unpack' is not defined

test_decorators.notes: Live function __dict__, staticmethod/classmethod __func__/__wrapped__ + attr delegation, and repr()-consistent !r/%r added. Remaining: test_dbcheck (eval of code object), test_errors (parser), test_eval_order (decorator arg count)

test_descr.notes: No longer panics: str subclasses now carry a backing string (Instance.BackingStr), str methods unwrap it, and ==/str()/len/index/in work. Remaining failures are deeper descriptor/metaclass features.

test_genexps.blocker: 0 tests ran (doctest-based or no unittest cases)

test_unpack_ex.blocker: 0 tests ran (doctest-based or no unittest cases)

test_iter.notes: Started running after BrokenIter added but is very slow

test_contextlib.notes: Runs. Jumped 39->51 when collections.deque truthiness was fixed (was always-truthy because IsTruthy required the full Object interface, which deque lacks): ExitStack's `while self._exit_callbacks:` looped forever -> 'pop from an empty deque'. deque is now iterable too.

infrastructure.test_package: test/ stub package at project root provides test.support, test.list_tests, test.seq_tests, test.support.{import_helper, os_helper, warnings_helper, script_helper}
infrastructure.cpython_source: tests/cpython-source/ (gitignored, clone of cpython v3.13.13 tag)
biggest_blockers_to_unblock_more: typing.Protocol __protocol_attrs__ (unblocks typing → inspect → dataclasses → doctest → test_iter, test_genexps, test_itertools, etc)
Parser support for @decorator with method receiving signatures it currently rejects (unblocks test_exceptions, test_with, test_call, test_builtin, test_generators, test_listcomps)
Pickle support for list iterators (unblocks test_iterator_pickle, test_reversed_pickle in test_list)

notes: Tests run directly from CPython 3.13.13 stdlib (tests/cpython-source/, gitignored).
test/ package at project root provides minimal infrastructure stubs so CPython test files can import test.support, test.list_tests, etc.
Many tests can run BECAUSE of these stubs but reveal M28 semantic gaps (descriptors, dict views, dunder protocols, etc).
Pre-stub baseline: 1 file passing (test_augassign). Current baseline: ~272 individual test methods passing across 9 files.
test_list went from 34/50 → 47/50 (May 30, 2026): ListInstance attrs, list subclass __init__/__new__, extended slice assignment, BigInt index clamping, context-aware list comparison.
test_dict went from ~36/123 → 90/123 (May 28, 2026): modCount mutation detection, __hash__ dispatch, classmethod fromkeys, cycle-detection repr, full __eq__ semantics.
